PREPOSITIONS OF PLACE
0%
Interactive English lesson · A1–A2

Prepositions of Place

Created by Viktoriia Pyniv.
AI-assisted educational resource developed with ChatGPT (OpenAI).
Resource: ESL Brains — Practising prepositions of place.

Listening Build the Room Speaking
10
useful location phrases
  • in, on, under
  • behind, next to, between, over
  • on the left/right, in the centre of

Listening

Start with meaning, then discover the words that show position.

1

Quick predictions

Look around.

Name three objects you can see.

Make a guess.

Where might people usually keep their keys?

Discuss.

Which object do people lose most often?

2

Listen and complete

Listen to the recording. Drag the words into the gaps, then check your answers.

Part 1 Objects A–D
A.The drop here is on the door.
B.The drop here is next to the door.
C.The drop here is in the bag.
D.The drop here is under the chair.
Part 2 Objects E–H
E.The drop here are behind the sofa.
F.The drop here is in the fridge.
G.The drop here is on the coffee table.
H.The drop here are between the photo and the plant.
3

What do these words mean?

Look at the pictures below. What do the words describe?

Build the Room

Place the objects, describe your choices and compare with a partner.

4

Drag, place and describe

wall floor

Drag items into the room. Double-click a placed item to return it to the word list.

📖 book
🧥 jacket
🔑 keys
👟 shoes
🍫 chocolate
🥤 water
🪴 plant
📱 phone
🖊️ pen
🎒 bag
💡 lamp
⚽ ball
🛏️ pillow
🖼️ picture
☕ cup
🧸 toy
📓 notebook
🎧 headphones
☂️ umbrella
📦 box
🖼️ photo
🧺 basket
⏰ alarm clock
💐 flowers
5

Tell your partner where you placed the objects.

Take turns describing your room. After each sentence, ask your partner where the same object is in their room.

Example:
A: A book is on the table. Where is your book?
B: My book is under the chair.

Find similarities and differences

6

Complete the room sentences.

More than one answer may be possible. Choose a preposition that makes sense.

Speaking

Use the language naturally, ask follow-up questions and discover something new about your partner.

7

Conversation cards

Question 1 of 20
Where is your TV?
Add details
